stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Peru's Chan Chan was built in the 1300s as the capital of the Chimor kingdom.
  • The city's adobe walls feature intricate relief sculptures of marine animals and geometric patterns.
  • Photometric stereo imaging is a recent technique that uses multiple light directions to reveal fine surface detail in photographs.
  • Archaeologist Manuel Cárdenas applied photometric stereo imaging to photographs he had taken of Chan Chan's eroded adobe walls.
  • The technique revealed dozens of relief sculptures that had been worn nearly smooth and were previously invisible to the naked eye.
stem: The student wants to present Cárdenas's research to an audience unfamiliar with Chan Chan. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Cárdenas photographed Chan Chan's adobe walls and then applied photometric stereo imaging to the photographs.
Using a recent imaging technique, Cárdenas revealed dozens of relief sculptures hidden on the walls of Chan Chan, a Peruvian city.
Photometric stereo imaging is a recent technique that Cárdenas used to reveal fine surface detail in photographs.
Built as the capital of the Chimor kingdom, Peru's Chan Chan has adobe walls that concealed worn relief sculptures.

rationale:
Choice B is the best answer because it briefly orients an unfamiliar audience to Chan Chan as a Peruvian city and then describes what Cárdenas found.

Choice A presupposes knowledge of the site. Choice C describes the technique but not the research. Choice D focuses on the site without giving the research result.
